fresh_facts.claim: On April 23, 2026, President Trump stated that the Strait of Hormuz will remain sealed until Iran agrees to a deal.
US forces seized multiple Iran-linked ships in the Indian Ocean, including the oil tanker Majestic X on Thursday, as part of over 30 turnarounds enforced by more than 30 US ships.
fresh_facts.source: Multiple sources reporting US naval operations

fresh_facts.claim: The USS George HW Bush carrier group is advancing toward the Persian Gulf.
Trump ordered the Navy to 'shoot and kill' Iranian boats laying mines while increasing demining efforts.
Trump claimed Iran's 159 naval ships are neutralized.
Israel's defense minister affirmed on April 23 that forces are ready to resume war if given a US green light.
An Israel-Lebanon ceasefire was extended by three weeks following Trump-announced peace talks.
Iran fired on and seized two cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz on April 23 as retaliation for the US blockade.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service with Iranian state media

fresh_facts.claim: Iranian state media released video of masked IRGC commandos boarding vessels during ship seizures.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting Iranian state media

fresh_facts.claim: Iran attacked three outbound ships on Wednesday prior to the April 23 ship seizures.
US intelligence notes Iran retains about half its ballistic missile stockpile, thousands of missiles, and one-way attack drones despite prior strikes.
Tehran refuses to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and vows continued responses to US actions.
Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf is pushing for diplomacy regarding US peace talks.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Iranian leadership

fresh_facts.claim: IRGC Commander Major General Ahmad Vahidi opposes US peace talks.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Iranian military leadership

fresh_facts.claim: Vahidi, with direct access to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, relays key decisions and has rising influence in decision-making.
fresh_facts.source: Israeli media and wire service reporting

fresh_facts.claim: Iranian officials have been unable to contact Supreme Leader Khamenei, hindering negotiations.
US President Donald Trump stated that time is running out for Iran, with deals only on US terms.
A ceasefire deadline between Iran and the US expired on April 22, 2026.
A power vacuum from unclear supreme leadership in Tehran emboldens hardliners and hinders ceasefire agreements.
Ghalibaf's diplomacy push aims to protect his position amid fears of IRGC consolidation.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Iranian politics

fresh_facts.claim: Exiled Prince Reza Pahlavi dismissed Iran-US negotiators as part of the repression system at a press conference in Berlin on Thursday.
Trump commented on Iran's internal 'infighting' between moderates and hardliners.

regime_fall.current_status: Direct military confrontation has escalated to active hostile engagement with Trump's shoot-to-kill orders, USS George HW Bush carrier group advancing, 30+ US ships enforcing blockade with seizures including oil tanker Majestic X, while Iran responds with Strait of Hormuz closures and vessel seizures. Internal Iranian paralysis deepens with Vahidi-Ghalibaf factional conflict, inability to contact Mojtaba Khamenei, and ceasefire deadline expiration on April 22 creating succession vacuum amid economic collapse.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On April 23-24, 2026, Iran fired on and seized two cargo vessels in the Strait of Hormuz as retaliation for US blockade operations, while Trump ordered Navy shoot-to-kill rules of engagement against Iranian mine-laying boats and declared time running out for Iran negotiations, with ceasefire deadline already expired on April 22.
